issues.title: Stale doc-comment on jumpToGate still says `[g] resume` after REV-001's fix to `[m] resume`
issues.description: update.go:212-213, the comment above jumpToGate() reads: "...the board move keys — [m] accept / [d] ship / [g] resume — act on it)...". REV-001 (review round 1) changed gateFor()'s decision-gate answer text from "[g] resume" to "[m] resume" (model.go ~line 541, confirmed fixed and correct in the running code and in the live-TUI capture). The comment above jumpToGate was not updated to match, so it now documents the pre-fix, wrong key. Not user-facing (a code comment only — the actual rendered strip row correctly reads `[m] resume` in every render captured, including a live tmux drive), but it will mislead the next reader into thinking `g` is a real board key.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE: update the comment at update.go:212-213 to read "...the board move keys — [m] accept / [d] ship / [m] resume — act on it)..." (or reword to avoid repeating `[m]` twice, e.g. "acts on it via the board's own key — [m] accept/go/resume, [d] ship").
